campbell.title: Campbell City Council — April 21, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to discuss and vote on changing the tax rates that businesses pay for their licenses in the city. Shannon Dragman from the finance department explained the proposed changes, and council members discussed the matter in public before making a decision.
keyTopics: Business license tax rates are being updated
Changes will take effect in the next fiscal year
Finance department presented the proposed rate revisions
Council deliberated and voted on the ordinance

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The City Council held a public hearing on March 18, 2026 to get community input on a new rule or policy. After hearing from residents, the council moved forward by introducing an ordinance (a new city law) and skipped the usual first reading step to speed up the process. They plan to vote on and approve the ordinance at their next meeting.
keyTopics: Public hearing held to gather resident feedback
New ordinance introduced to city council
First reading waived to expedite the approval process
Ordinance scheduled for final vote at next regular meeting

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — April 21, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met on April 21, 2026, to approve meeting minutes from earlier in the month and review the town's finances for February. The council also authorized the Town Manager to sign several contracts for construction work and professional services.
keyTopics: Approved minutes from previous closed and public meetings
Reviewed February 2026 financial and investment report
Authorized construction contracts with QLM, Inc. and other firms
Approved professional services agreement (3-year term)

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 29, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council held a meeting to organize their upcoming agendas for May 5th and May 12th, 2026. They reviewed what items should be added to these meetings, decided which topics needed specific time slots, and checked paperwork requirements. The council also reviewed public records from mid-to-late April.
keyTopics: Finalized agenda for May 5, 2026 council meeting
Reviewed draft agenda for May 12, 2026 meeting
Assigned time slots to specific agenda items
Checked notice and waiver requirements for proper procedures
Reviewed public records from April 16-23, 2026

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 30, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council met to approve meeting minutes and hear from residents during public comment. The main focus was deciding which neighborhood improvement projects would receive funding for the next fiscal year, with council members discussing their recommendations and attending neighborhood meetings.
keyTopics: Approval of previous meeting minutes
Public comment period for residents
Neighborhood grant funding decisions for 2026-27 budget
Council attendance at neighborhood meetings
Staff and committee reports and questions

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — April 28, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale City Council held a special evening meeting on April 28, 2026, to interview candidates for various city boards and commissions. Residents were able to watch and participate both in person and online during the public comment period.
keyTopics: Board and Commission candidate interviews
Public comment opportunity (in-person and online)
Special study session format

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — April 30, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council held a meeting on April 30, 2026, primarily focused on reviewing and approving meeting minutes from the Economic Development Committee's January and March 2026 sessions. The council also opened the floor for residents to speak about any topics within the committee's jurisdiction for up to three minutes each.
keyTopics: Approval of January 29, 2026 Economic Development Committee minutes
Approval of March 27, 2026 Economic Development Committee minutes
Public comment period for residents on economic development matters

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 21, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The Santa Clara City Council met to discuss putting up large promotional signs around Levi's Stadium for the 2026 FIFA World Cup soccer tournament. The council also approved a new contract with the city's firefighters union covering 2026-2029, though one agenda item was withdrawn before the meeting.
keyTopics: Temporary large signs around Levi's Stadium during World Cup 2026
New firefighters union contract for 2026-2029
One agenda item withdrawn by staff

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 21, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council held a meeting on April 21, 2026 to make decisions about the city's budget and finances for the next year, review rules for various fees and services, and discuss plans to address homelessness in Santa Clara County. The council also approved meeting minutes and reviewed upcoming agenda items.
keyTopics: Approved the city's budget and development plans for fiscal year 2026-27
Reviewed tax and fiscal responsibility policies
Updated user fees and service charges
Discussed Santa Clara County's homelessness prevention plan
Approved previous meeting minutes and scheduled upcoming council meetings

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — April 29, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council met to discuss several items including routine administrative matters and a major decision about a vacant lot on Old Trace Road. The council considered a proposal to split the 1-acre property into 9 new single-family homes and 7 junior accessory dwelling units (smaller secondary units), along with a private street to serve them.
keyTopics: Proposed subdivision of 4103 Old Trace Road into 9 homes plus 7 small secondary units
Environmental review exemption approved under state housing law
Election of new council leadership (Chair and Vice Chair positions)
Property zoned for residential estate use in Palo Alto
